guideline_references: RACGP / Osteoporosis Australia — Vitamin D and health in adults (Australian position statement)
Therapeutic Guidelines: Bone and metabolism — osteomalacia / vitamin D deficiency
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who palpated bone tenderness, graded proximal power (3/5), administered oral tramadol, and arranged next-morning bloods and radiographs. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate, grade power, take bloods (25-OH vitamin D, calcium, phosphate, ALP, PTH) or obtain radiographs; it is scored on recognising the metabolic-bone-disease pattern, arranging urgent labs + imaging, arranging urgent in-person assessment for the fall / unexcludable occult fracture, giving fall-safety advice, and NOT prescribing an opioid or a vitamin D dose remotely.

De-anchoring: the source prints a diagnostic tetrad (diffuse bone pain, proximal muscle weakness, bone tenderness on palpation, elevated ALP / Looser zones) and names the diagnosis up front. That tetrad + the diagnosis label + the anticipated lab/radiograph confirmation are the sealed answer key (nodes 10/11) and do NOT appear in nodes 01/02.

Tier discipline: the acute complication (a low-impact fall with inability to mobilise and an occult insufficiency / neck-of-femur fracture that cannot be excluded remotely) is tiered, not the chronic label. Source Risk Level is Environmental/Social=High with an urgent same-day in-person disposition (not ED transfer tonight) and no current patient-reportable time-critical emergency feature — AI-correct tier is T3 (urgent same-day in-person + labs), with T5/000 escalation edges for acute fracture, cauda equina and symptomatic hypocalcaemic tetany.
Age inferred as older_adult and sex_at_birth inferred as female (frail, chronically ill-appearing, housebound, adult child present, low body weight) — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source case EMD-031 (Osteomalacia).
